question: [Q1] What is DBMS and what are its main advantages? (Focusing on TCS interview pattern - Set 1)
correctAnswer: A Database Management System is software to manage databases. Advantages include data sharing, security, integration, and recovery.
explanation: DBMS provides a systematic way to store, retrieve, and manage data.

question: [Q2] Explain the difference between Primary Key and Unique Key. (Focusing on TCS interview pattern - Set 1)
correctAnswer: A Primary Key uniquely identifies a record and cannot be NULL. A Unique Key also ensures uniqueness but allows one NULL value.
explanation: Each table can have only one Primary Key but multiple Unique Keys.

question: [Q3] What is normalisation in SQL database design? (Focusing on TCS interview pattern - Set 1)
correctAnswer: Normalisation is the process of structuring relational databases to reduce data redundancy and improve data integrity.
explanation: Common normal forms are 1NF, 2NF, 3NF, and BCNF.

question: [Q4] Explain SDLC and list its major phases. (Focusing on TCS interview pattern - Set 1)
correctAnswer: Software Development Life Cycle is a structured process to build software. Phases: Requirement Analysis, Design, Coding, Testing, Deployment, Maintenance.
explanation: Different models include Waterfall, Agile, Scrum, Spiral.

question: [Q5] What is an index in SQL and why is it used? (Focusing on TCS interview pattern - Set 1)
correctAnswer: An index is a performance-tuning method that allows faster retrieval of records from a table.
explanation: Indexes speed up SELECT queries but slow down INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE queries.

question: [Q39] What is object-oriented programming (OOP)? List core principles. (Focusing on INFOSYS interview pattern - Set 1)
correctAnswer: OOP is a programming paradigm based on 'objects'. Core principles: Inheritance, Polymorphism, Encapsulation, and Abstraction.
explanation: OOP mirrors real-world items and structures code for reusability.

question: [Q40] What is the difference between list and tuple in Python? (Focusing on INFOSYS interview pattern - Set 1)
correctAnswer: Lists are mutable (can be changed); tuples are immutable (cannot be changed after creation).
explanation: Lists use square brackets []; tuples use parentheses ().

question: [Q41] Explain method overloading vs method overriding in Java. (Focusing on INFOSYS interview pattern - Set 1)
correctAnswer: Overloading: same method name but different parameters in the same class (compile-time). Overriding: redefine a superclass method in subclass (runtime).
explanation: Overloading is static polymorphism; overriding is dynamic polymorphism.

question: [Q42] What is a RESTful API? (Focusing on INFOSYS interview pattern - Set 1)
correctAnswer: A RESTful API is an interface that uses HTTP requests (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) to manage data using stateless operations.
explanation: REST stands for Representational State Transfer.

question: [Q43] Explain the concepts of stack and heap memory in Java. (Focusing on INFOSYS interview pattern - Set 1)
correctAnswer: Stack memory is used for static memory allocation and thread execution. Heap memory is used for dynamic memory allocation of objects.
explanation: Stack memory is fast and local; heap memory is large and shared.

question: [Q44] What is object-oriented programming (OOP)? List core principles. (Focusing on INFOSYS interview pattern - Set 2)
correctAnswer: OOP is a programming paradigm based on 'objects'. Core principles: Inheritance, Polymorphism, Encapsulation, and Abstraction.
explanation: OOP mirrors real-world items and structures code for reusability.

question: [Q77] What is Cloud Computing and what are the main service models? (Focusing on ACCENTURE interview pattern - Set 1)
correctAnswer: Delivery of computing services over the internet. Service models: IaaS (Infrastructure), PaaS (Platform), SaaS (Software).
explanation: Examples: AWS, Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud Platform.

question: [Q78] What is Git and how does it differ from SVN? (Focusing on ACCENTURE interview pattern - Set 1)
correctAnswer: Git is a distributed version control system (every developer has a local clone). SVN is a centralized version control system.
explanation: Git supports branch operations and offline work much better than SVN.

question: [Q79] Explain the Agile software development methodology. (Focusing on ACCENTURE interview pattern - Set 1)
correctAnswer: Agile is an iterative approach to software development and project management that helps teams deliver value faster and with fewer headaches.
explanation: Agile values collaboration, working software, and responding to change.

question: [Q80] What is a firewall in network security? (Focusing on ACCENTURE interview pattern - Set 1)
correctAnswer: A firewall is a network security system that monitors and controls incoming and outgoing network traffic based on predetermined security rules.
explanation: Firewalls establish a barrier between trusted internal networks and untrusted external ones.

question: [Q81] What is the difference between SQL and NoSQL? (Focusing on ACCENTURE interview pattern - Set 1)
correctAnswer: SQL databases are relational, table-based, and structured. NoSQL databases are non-relational, document/key-value based, and flexible.
explanation: SQL scales vertically (ACID); NoSQL scales horizontally (CAP theorem).

question: [Q102] What is Cloud Computing and what are the main service models? (Focusing on ACCENTURE interview pattern - Set 6)
correctAnswer: Delivery of computing services over the internet. Service models: IaaS (Infrastructure), PaaS (Platform), SaaS (Software).
explanation: Examples: AWS, Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud Platform.

question: [Q103] What is Git and how does it differ from SVN? (Focusing on ACCENTURE interview pattern - Set 6)
correctAnswer: Git is a distributed version control system (every developer has a local clone). SVN is a centralized version control system.
explanation: Git supports branch operations and offline work much better than SVN.

question: [Q104] Explain the Agile software development methodology. (Focusing on ACCENTURE interview pattern - Set 6)
correctAnswer: Agile is an iterative approach to software development and project management that helps teams deliver value faster and with fewer headaches.
explanation: Agile values collaboration, working software, and responding to change.

question: [Q105] What is a firewall in network security? (Focusing on ACCENTURE interview pattern - Set 6)
correctAnswer: A firewall is a network security system that monitors and controls incoming and outgoing network traffic based on predetermined security rules.
explanation: Firewalls establish a barrier between trusted internal networks and untrusted external ones.

question: [Q106] What is the difference between SQL and NoSQL? (Focusing on ACCENTURE interview pattern - Set 6)
correctAnswer: SQL databases are relational, table-based, and structured. NoSQL databases are non-relational, document/key-value based, and flexible.
explanation: SQL scales vertically (ACID); NoSQL scales horizontally (CAP theorem).

question: [Q115] What is an Operating System and what are its core tasks? (Focusing on WIPRO interview pattern - Set 1)
correctAnswer: An OS is system software that manages computer hardware and software resources. Core tasks: memory management, process scheduling, file system, I/O management.
explanation: Examples: Windows, Linux, macOS.

question: [Q116] Explain the OSI Reference Model and name its 7 layers. (Focusing on WIPRO interview pattern - Set 1)
correctAnswer: It standardizes network communication functions. Layers: Physical, Data Link, Network, Transport, Session, Presentation, Application.
explanation: Use the mnemonic: 'Please Do Not Throw Sausage Pizza Away'.

question: [Q117] What is a Pointer in C++? (Focusing on WIPRO interview pattern - Set 1)
correctAnswer: A pointer is a variable that stores the memory address of another variable.
explanation: Pointers enable direct memory manipulation and dynamic allocation.

question: [Q118] What is the difference between a Process and a Thread? (Focusing on WIPRO interview pattern - Set 1)
correctAnswer: A process is an execution of a program (isolated memory). A thread is a path of execution within a process (shared memory).
explanation: Creating threads is faster and consumes fewer resources than creating processes.

question: [Q119] What is a virtual function in C++ and why is it used? (Focusing on WIPRO interview pattern - Set 1)
correctAnswer: A virtual function is a member function in a base class that you expect to redefine in derived classes. Used to achieve runtime polymorphism.
explanation: Declared using the 'virtual' keyword.
